title: `_curses._mk_acs` with invalid `ichar` segfaults
author: devdanzin
body:
It's possible to make PyPy segfault by calling `_curses._mk_acs("", "\U0010ffff")`:

```python
Python 3.10.14 (6b367d9e6715, Nov 14 2024, 04:48:15)
[PyPy 7.3.18-alpha0 with GCC 10.2.1 20210130 (Red Hat 10.2.1-11)] on linux
Type "help", "copyright", "credits" or "license" for more information.
And now for something completely different: ``All problems in computer science
can be solved by another level of indirection. --Butler Lampson''
>>>> import _curses
>>>> _curses._mk_acs("", "\U0010ffff")
Segmentation fault (core dumped)
```

Found using fusil by @vstinner.

comments.author: mattip
comments.body: fixed in 5cccf8d934d by hiding the helper function better. It is not meant to be exposed to end users.
